NetworkSignal.title: NetworkSignal
NetworkSignal.description: Represents a network signal data point with its location and strength.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NetworkSignal entity.

latitude.description: The latitude of the network signal data point.

longitude.description: The longitude of the network signal data point.

signalStrength.description: The signal strength at the given latitude and longitude, ranging from 0 to 100.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the network signal measurement.

definition.description: Collection to store network signal data, including location, strength, and timestamp.

params.name: networkSignalId
params.description: Unique identifier for the NetworkSignal document.

firestore.reasoning: The data structure is designed to store network signal data collected from a mobile app for visualization on an interactive map. The core entity is `NetworkSignal`, which includes latitude, longitude, signal strength, and a timestamp.  Since the app's primary function is to display this data without complex user-specific access controls, all data is stored in a single collection named `network_signals`. This simplifies data retrieval for the map visualization. This approach ensures Authorization Independence is not a concern, as there are no user-specific or role-based access restrictions required for this data. QAPs are straightforward because all documents in `network_signals` are accessible for reading and visualization. The structure prioritizes ease of access and visualization over granular access control, which aligns with the crowdsourced nature of the data collection.
